stmt: ∀ {α : Type*} [ACR α] [ACR.Prov α] [ACR.Reft α] [ACR.APS α] (k : ℕ), 0 < k → (∃ x : α, x ≤ ((fun z : α => □z)^[k]) x ∧ Set.Finite {y : α | ∃ n : ℕ, ((((fun z : α => □z)^[k])^[n]) x = y)}) → Nonempty (ACR.HenkinFixpoint α)
priority_rationale: This is a useful composite-step bridge from finite box^k orbit data to Henkin existence, but it is still a local support lemma unless positive-iterate collapse is settled.

stmt: ∀ {α : Type*} [ACR α] [ACR.Prov α] [ACR.Reft α] [ACR.APS α] (k : ℕ), 0 < k → Nonempty (ACR.GödelFixpoint α) → (∃ g : ACR.GödelFixpoint α, ∃ m n : ℕ, m < n ∧ ((((fun z : α => □z)^[k])^[m]) g.1 ≐ (((fun z : α => □z)^[k])^[n]) g.1)) → Nonempty (ACR.HenkinFixpoint α)
priority_rationale: It extends the proved one-step repeat criterion to positive iterates on Gödel orbits and would materially support the composite-dynamics frontier without itself giving the full collapse theorem.

stmt: ∀ {α : Type*} [ACR α] [ACR.Prov α] [ACR.Reft α] [ACR.APS α], ACR.Consistent α → Nonempty (ACR.GödelFixpoint α) → ¬ Nonempty (ACR.HenkinFixpoint α) → ∀ (g : ACR.GödelFixpoint α) (k m n : ℕ), 0 < k → m < n → ¬ ((((fun z : α => □z)^[k])^[m]) g.1 ≐ (((fun z : α => □z)^[k])^[n]) g.1)
priority_rationale: This is a sharp separator-side obstruction for box^k orbit repetition, but it mainly packages a contrapositive constraint rather than changing the summary on its own.

stmt: ∀ {α : Type*} [ACR α] [ACR.Prov α] [ACR.Reft α] [ACR.APS α] (k : ℕ), 0 < k → (∃ x : α, x ≐ ((fun z : α => □z)^[k]) x) → Nonempty (ACR.HenkinFixpoint α)
priority_rationale: A fixed-k implication from box^k fixed points to Henkin points directly attacks the main collapse bottleneck, but it is still a fixed-k local instance of the broader target.

stmt: ∃ (α : Type*) (_ : ACR α) (_ : ACR.Prov α) (_ : ACR.Reft α) (_ : ACR.APS α), ACR.Consistent α ∧ Nonempty (ACR.GödelFixpoint α) ∧ ¬(⊠⊠(⊤ : α) ≐ ⊠⊤)
priority_rationale: A consistent Gödel-without-top-box-equation model would be meaningful boundary evidence below C5, but it is a one-sided separator rather than the exact bridge theorem.
